title: Labor-Market Research, Role Families, and Data Limits · Career Evidence Triage
summary: A realistic labor-market research, role families, and data limits artifact contains one plausible targeting, evidence, integrity, access, privacy, process, or decision defect. Locate the first unsupported claim before revising it. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in dated labor-market evidence brief and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish occupations, job titles, functions, industries, products, seniority, and employment arrangements when mapping possible work.
Record source owner, geography, population, collection period, update date, method, and missing groups before using labor data.
Interpret posting-frequency signals as bounded demand evidence rather than required-skill, hiring-probability, or future-certainty claims.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# job-search cumulative learner-controlled career campaign
# Browser labs review fictional evidence contracts only. They never contact people,
# submit applications, access accounts, expose personal data, negotiate, or accept offers.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-learner
target-role: [replace with role family, work outcomes, and bounded level]
jurisdiction: [replace with location and current official guidance boundary]
audience: [replace with reviewer need, time, format, and access context]
claim: [replace with one truthful role-relevant behavior claim]
evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, observation, changed case, contribution, and limit]
process-state: [replace with discover, investigate, apply, interview, offer, close, or archive]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
privacy-consent: [replace with purpose, minimization, permission, retention, deletion, and safe contact]
decision: [replace with learner-owned next decision and stop rule]
transfer-boundary: learner-approved external actions only
review:

steps.title: Order the regression proof · Debug Role Hypothesis
steps.instruction:
Order the Role Hypothesis evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Order the regression proof · Debug Role Hypothesis.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Role Hypothesis assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Order the regression proof · Debug Role Hypothesis, keep Role Hypothesis connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.text: A viral salary post combines national medians, remote listings, senior titles, old data, and one technology trend into a guaranteed entry-level forecast. The debug variation 5 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Select a small, testable role-family hypothesis from personal constraints, evidence adjacency, real tasks, and market signals.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Role Hypothesis has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: For Order the regression proof · Debug Role Hypothesis, name the learner goal, target role, jurisdiction, audience, truthful claim, contribution, source date, evidence revision, accessibility, privacy, consent, process state, decision owner, non-claim, and transfer boundary, Role Hypothesis precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Order the regression proof · Debug Role Hypothesis, trace this concrete input and predict targeting, source, evidence, contribution, access, privacy, consent, process, decision, rejection, repair, follow-up, and learner-controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: A viral salary post combines national medians, remote listings, senior titles, old data, and one technology trend into a guaranteed entry-level forecast. The debug variation 5 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Select a small, testable role-family hypothesis from personal constraints, evidence adjacency, real tasks, and market signals.

For Order the regression proof · Debug Role Hypothesis, challenge the documented misconception with changed-candidate, target-role, source-date, jurisdiction, audience, access, privacy, consent, process-state, interview, offer, rejection, repair, and learner-controlled transfer evidence: Keeping every possible role open always increases opportunity.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints for the debug Role Hypothesis case
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure for the debug Role Hypothesis case
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence for the debug Role Hypothesis case
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise for the debug Role Hypothesis case

stimulus.title: Occupation Vs Title case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A viral salary post combines national medians, remote listings, senior titles, old data, and one technology trend into a guaranteed entry-level forecast. The debug variation 6 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Distinguish occupations, job titles, functions, industries, products, seniority, and employment arrangements when mapping possible work.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: The same title names the same work at every organization.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish occupations, job titles, functions, industries, products, seniority, and employment arrangements when mapping possible work. Artifact under review: dated labor-market evidence brief.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Occupation Vs Title, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Occupation Vs Title record says dated labor-market evidence brief looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the inspect the failure record evidence boundary for Occupation Vs Title.

For inspect the failure record, apply Occupation Vs Title without its stated preconditions, omit the dated labor-market evidence brief trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Occupation Vs Title for dated labor-market evidence brief.
